sections.paragraph:
κατακόπτω impf. κατέκοπτον; fut. κατακόψω LXX; 1 aor. κατέκοψα LXX. Pass.: aor. κατεκόπην (LXX; JosAs 23:3); pf. ptc. κατακεκομμένος LXX (Hdt.+).

① to cut in a rough manner, lacereate, cut τινά τινι someone w. someth. ἑαυτὸν λίθοις cut oneself w. stones Mk 5:5 (REB: ‘gash himself’). Some prefer the sense beat (Kaibel 316, 3 of a wailing woman beating her breasts, s. κόπτω: PLips 37, 20 κατέκοψαν πληγαῖς αὐτόν; PSI 313, 10. S. other exx. in Field, Notes 27).

② break in pieces (Pla., Euthyd. 301c; 2 Ch 15:16; 34:7) of stones (Is 27:9) Hv 3, 2, 7; cp. 3, 6, 1.—M-M.

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

REB REB = Revised English Bible—List 6

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

PLips PLips = Griech. Urkunden der Papyrussammlung zu Leipzig—List 4

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

exx. exx. = examples

Field, Notes Field, Notes = Notes on the Translation of the NT

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Hv Hv = Visions

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 518
